categories.label: Compute & Storage
categories.description: Virtual machines, block storage, and persistent volumes.

categories.label: Application, Data & Observability
categories.description: Managed databases, observability stacks, and app platforms.

categories.label: Secure Connectivity
categories.description: VPNs, private endpoints, and zero-trust network access.

categories.label: Infrastructure Security
categories.description: Key management, secrets, certificates, and policy engines.

providers.name: KubeVirt
providers.summary: Run virtual machines natively on Kubernetes.
providers.description: KubeVirt extends Kubernetes by adding additional virtualization resource types through Kubernetes CRDs. By using this mechanism, the KubeVirt community is able to deliver a comprehensive solution for VM management alongside containers.

providers.name: VMware vSphere
providers.summary: Manage vSphere VMs and resources declaratively.
providers.description: The vSphere Crossplane provider enables GitOps-based management of VMware vSphere infrastructure. Declare your virtual machines, datastores, and networks as Kubernetes resources.

providers.name: OpenStack
providers.summary: Provision and manage OpenStack cloud resources declaratively.
providers.description: The OpenStack Crossplane provider enables GitOps-driven management of OpenStack infrastructure — compute instances, networks, security groups, volumes, and object storage — through Kubernetes-native APIs.

providers.name: Grafana
providers.summary: Manage Grafana dashboards, alerts, and data sources via Crossplane.
providers.description: The Grafana Crossplane provider enables declarative management of Grafana resources — dashboards, folders, alert rules, contact points, data sources, and teams — through Kubernetes-native APIs. GitOps your entire observability stack alongside your infrastructure.

providers.name: Helm
providers.summary: Deploy and manage Helm releases as Crossplane managed resources.
providers.description: The Helm Crossplane provider lets platform teams declaratively manage Helm chart releases across any Kubernetes cluster. Define releases, values, and namespaces as Kubernetes resources and reconcile them through standard GitOps workflows.

providers.name: Kubernetes
providers.summary: Manage Kubernetes objects across clusters as Crossplane resources.
providers.description: The Kubernetes Crossplane provider enables declarative management of any Kubernetes resource — across any cluster — as a Crossplane managed resource. Use it to compose multi-cluster deployments, manage RBAC, and provision workload resources from a central control plane.

providers.name: Aviatrix
providers.summary: Declaratively manage Aviatrix multi-cloud network connectivity.
providers.description: The Aviatrix Crossplane provider enables GitOps-based management of Aviatrix networking resources — transit gateways, spoke gateways, VPN connections, and segmentation domains — across AWS, Azure, GCP, and private cloud targets.

providers.name: Vault
providers.summary: Manage HashiCorp Vault secrets, policies, and auth methods declaratively.
providers.description: The Vault Crossplane provider enables GitOps-driven management of HashiCorp Vault resources — secret engines, policies, auth methods, PKI roles, and identity groups — as Kubernetes-native managed resources.

providers.name: AzureAD
providers.summary: Manage Azure Active Directory identities and access policies.
providers.description: The AzureAD Crossplane provider enables declarative management of Azure Active Directory resources — applications, service principals, groups, role assignments, and conditional access policies — through Kubernetes-native APIs.

providers.name: Terraform
providers.summary: Manage any Terraform module as a Crossplane managed resource.
providers.description: The Upbound Terraform provider bridges Crossplane and Terraform, allowing platform teams to wrap any Terraform module as a Kubernetes-native Crossplane managed resource. Combine GitOps workflows with the full Terraform ecosystem — providers, modules, and workspaces — all managed declaratively.
